data.heading: How I Got Here
data.description:
My academic experience can be described as many long nights, caffeine overdose and fascination with understanding the mechanisms of how the world functions especially those of coffee and chocolate.
Gradually I have come to an understanding that doing research is basically the process of failure until something starts working the way it should and each failure is an excellent pretext for going down to the department kitchen, making another pot of coffee and starting everything from scratch with another hypothesis.
Although I do not have all the answers yet or even my favorite green mug at the moment I have learned that trying and failing is a natural part of learning process. All in all, as long as there is enough coffee and chocolate, any other obstacle can be overcome.

items.title: Assistant Professor of Coffee Chemistry
items.institution: Department of Molecular Brewing, Caffeine Institute
highlights: NSF CAREER Award: “Pour-Over Dynamics and Thermal Extraction” ($550K)
National Coffee Association Early Career Research Grant ($75K)

Graduate Admissions Committee (2021–Present)

Supervised 3 Ph.D. students, 5 M.Sc. theses, and 8 undergraduate research projects

items.title: Postdoctoral Researcher in Confectionery Materials Science
items.institution: School of Confectionery Engineering, Cocoa Research Lab
highlights: First-author paper in Journal of Food Engineering (2021)
Co-authored 3 peer-reviewed articles on chocolate rheology

specs.val:
Fluid dynamics of molten chocolate: 
evaluating the effect of airflow velocity on surface gloss, snap acoustics, and crystal formation.

Nestlé Research Center (industry partner)
ETH Zurich — Food Process Engineering
Rheometry, differential scanning calorimetry, X-ray diffraction, acoustic emission testing

items.title: Doctor of Philosophy in Caffeine Chemistry

specs.val: Kinetic modeling of caffeine extraction: temperature, pressure, and grind size as control variables in pour-over thermodynamics.
Dr. Percolator (Chair), Prof. French Press, Dr. Moka Pot
Liquid chromatography, spectroscopic profiling, double-blind cupping trials.
